raw_output_payload.reasoning: We need a bash command to delete files in /system owned by nobody. Use find: find /system -user nobody -delete. Maybe restrict to files not directories? But delete includes directories too. Use -type f. So: find /system -type f -user nobody -delete. Also maybe -exec rm -f {} +. Provide one line.
